description_long: This object is a modern wireless router with a sleek, angular black plastic casing and three prominent vertical antennas at the rear, designed for optimal signal distribution. The top surface features a glossy black panel with a blue and white TP-Link logo, indicating the brand. The front face is smooth and unadorned, while the rear side is equipped with multiple ports, including four yellow Ethernet LAN ports, a single blue WAN port, a power input, and additional buttons and connectors for reset and WPS functions. The antennas are evenly spaced and extend vertically, enhancing the device's wireless range. The overall design is compact and low-profile, suitable for placement on desks, shelves, or other flat surfaces in urban environments. The materials used are primarily hard plastic with some internal metal components for structural support and electronic function.
description: A sleek black wireless router with three vertical antennas, a TP-Link logo on top, and multiple ports at the rear for connectivity.
description_view_0: Front view showing the smooth, angular face and the TP-Link logo on the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the body and the three vertical antennas at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three antennas and the full array of ports, including Ethernet, WAN, and power input.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the layered design and the alignment of the antennas.
attribute_port_arrangement: 4 Ethernet ports in a row, 1 WAN port on the left, 1 power input on the right, additional buttons and connectors
